data.title: Bayer subsidiary gains worldwide rights to develop cancer asset VVD-214
data.description:
• Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) subsidiary Vividion Therapeutics has gained the worldwide rights to develop and commercialize the Werner helicase covalent inhibitor VVD-214.
  • The asset has been developed between Vividion and Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) under a collaboration and license agreement inked in 2020.
  • A phase 1 trial currently under way is examining VVD-214 as monotherapy and in combination with Keytruda (pembrolizumab) for patients with solid tumors that display high MSI or deficient mismatch repair including colorectal, endometrial, ovarian, and gastric cancers.
  • Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) noted that Werner helicase is" a DNA repair enzyme and a highly sought-after synthetic lethal target for treatment of cancers with microsatellite instability. By inhibiting WRN, VVD-214 aims to cause lethal DNA damage in cancers with high microsatellite instability while minimizing harm to healthy cells."

data.title: Trump again urges Powell to cut rates after soft private sector job growth
data.description:
President Donald Trump once again urged Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell to lower interest rates after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years. 

U.S. private sector employment grew by 37K in May, missing the +110K consensus and cooling from the 62K increase in April, according to data released by ADP on Wednesday. It was the lowest monthly job total from the ADP count since March 2023. 

Trump's latest criticism of Powell comes about a week after he met the central bank chief "to discuss economic developments, including growth, employment, and inflation".

"ADP NUMBER OUT!!! 'Too Late' Powell must now LOWER THE RATE. He is unbelievable!!! Europe has lowered NINE TIMES!" Trump posted on Truth Social. 

Trump has been ramping up his criticism of Powell for being too slow to cut interest rates, and there were reports of him considering Powell's termination. He later said he had "no intention" of removing Powell.

Last month, Trump had called Powell a "fool" a day after the central bank held its key interest rate steady, as widely expected.

The Fed reduced its federal funds rate target range by 100 basis points starting in December but has held the rates at its current range of 4.25%-4.50% since the beginning of 2025.

Powell's term as chair expires in May 2026.

Stock index futures earlier ticked lower after the ADP data but then recovered. Wall Street subsequently opened with slight gains.  

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Post Holdings earns high marks from analysts for its 8th Avenue Food & Provisions takeover
data.description:
Post Holdings (NYSE:POST) is being sized up by analysts after its deal to acquire 8th Avenue Food & Provisions for ~$880 million. The St. Louis-based company said the acquisition internalizes the manufacturing of Post's (NYSE:POST) Peter Pan peanut butter, represents its entry into the dry pasta category with leading brand Ronzoni, and enables greater participation in the growing granola sub-category of ready-to-eat cereal. 

8th Avenue was established in October 2018 as a strategic blend of Post Holdings’ private brand businesses, in partnership with the private equity firm Thomas H. Lee Partners. The company has grown through several key acquisitions, including a peanut butter processing plant in Illinois and the Ronzoni pasta brand. Of note, Post Holdings (NYSE:POST) already held a 60.5% ownership stake before announcing the complete takeover.

The acquisition is expected to be completed by July 1

Evercore ISI boosted its price target on Post Holdings (POST) to $131 following the deal. "Over time, we can imagine the nut butter business as a foundation for further organic growth (e.g. high protein) and a platform for synergistic acquisitions," wrote analyst David Palmer.

Mizuho Securities analyst John Baumgartner said positives from the deal include that Post Holdings (POST) knows 8th Avenue well, which limits execution risk. The steady financial returns and free cash flow accretion potential were also seen as positive. Notably, the pro forma net leverage 4.6X should not preclude additional M&A from the food company, per Baumgartner. Mizuho kept an Outperform rating on Post (POST). 

Wells Fargo analyst Marc Torrente highlighted that Post (POST) is a known entity providing scale in complementary categories/price-points that enhance the portfolio. "Strategically and financially seems like a good use of capital, with flexibility to continue opportunistic allocation," he noted.

Shares of Post Holdings (POST) were up 1.0% in early trading on Wednesday. On a year-to-date basis, the food stock is down 3.7%. Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts have a consensus Buy rating on POST. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating is also flashing Buy.

data.title: Neogen falls after Q4 update
data.description:
Neogen Corporation (NASDAQ:NEOG) lost ~14% in the morning hours on Wednesday after the food safety player updated its Q4 fiscal 2025 outlook in conjunction with a presentation at the ongoing William Blair Growth Stock Conference in Chicago, Illinois.

While the Lansing, Michigan-based healthcare supplier cited preliminary data to announce that its Q4 revenue will approach guidance, the company projected temporary headwinds affecting its gross margin.

However, NEOG said its adjusted EBITDA is expected to hit the high teens range even as market conditions remain weak, with Q3 softness extending to Q4.

The company also projected nearly $5M in impact related to tariffs and announced plans to market its genomics business as the second divestiture.

In April, Neogen (NASDAQ:NEOG) agreed to sell its global cleaners and disinfectants business to Kersia Group. The deal worth $130M remains on track to close in Q4 FY26, the company said.

data.title: Rising Treasuries could push the S&P 500 to 6,200 – UBS
data.description:
U.S. equities (SP500) could rally to around 6200 points if the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) reaches 5%, according to Gerry Fowler, chief of European Equity Strategy and head of Global Derivatives Strategy at UBS.

“We see the S&P 500 (SP500) trading up to 6,200 on moderate (mid-teen) realized volatility,” Fowler said, noting this would represent a significant upside from current levels despite the seemingly counterintuitive relationship between higher yields and equity performance.

The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield (US10Y) serves as a proxy for the global risk-free rate and is widely used in discounted cash flow modeling, making its movements crucial for equity valuations.

“The UST yield is not an island,” he said, and added that yield changes are “almost always coincident with changes in the other components of equity returns, including risk premia and cash flow forecasts.”

Term premia risks represent a particular concern for investors, as they could drive yields higher without the usual offsetting positive effects on growth expectations.

“The sharp rise in term premia in recent years is a special case of UST yields moving without much effect or correlation with other components of the discount rate,” Fowler added.

He identified several factors driving this dynamic, including “uncertainty, deficits, debt burdens, and potential disincentives for U.S. inbound portfolio flows.”

A more optimistic scenario envisions 5% yields resulting from continued economic resilience, with tariff revenue supporting fiscal stimulus and budget proposals successfully incentivizing investment.

“We assume that 5% yields come from a rise of around 32 basis points in 10-year real yields and a 15 basis points rise in 10-year inflation expectations,” Fowler said. This growth-oriented outlook could lift both yields and profit expectations simultaneously, creating a more favorable environment for equities despite higher rates.

Sector performance would likely diverge significantly in such a scenario, with cyclicals outperforming defensive stocks.

“Our models show potential for outperformance from airlines (AIRL), (JETS), mining (XME), (PICK), (ICOP), and consumer discretionary stocks (XLY), while defensives like consumer staples (XLP) and health care (XLV) stagnate,” Fowler said, and added that gold miners (GDX), (GDXJ) could be “flat to down on this growth-oriented outlook.”

More U.S. government bond ETFs: (GOVI), (SPTL), (TIP), (SPTI), (BIL)

Investors can track other bond ETFs here.

data.title: GeoPark adopts rights plan after investor buys 10% of shares in two weeks
data.description:
GeoPark (NYSE:GPRK) said late Tuesday it adopted a limited duration shareholder rights plan intended to protect it from potential hostile takeovers after a single stockholder accumulated a greater than 10% stake in the company's shares.

Pampa Energia (NYSE:PAM) subsidiary Generacion Argentina purchased 5.2M shares in GeoPark (NYSE:GPRK) during the May 15-30 period, amounting to 10.2% of the Colombian oil and gas operator's shares.

Under the plan, the rights will become exercisable if any entity or person acquires ownership of 12% or more of GeoPark's (GPRK) shares in a transaction not approved by the board, and each right would then entitle its holder to purchase additional shares at the then-current price, but the shares would have a market value of twice the exercise price of the right.

data.title: Microsoft offers free cybersecurity protection for European governments: report
data.description:
• Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) is offering a new cybersecurity protection to European governments for free to help defend themselves from cyber threats, the tech giant said.
  • “If we can bring more to Europe of what we have developed in the United States, that will strengthen cybersecurity protection for more European institutions,” Microsoft President Brad Smith told Reuters. “You're going to see other things we are doing later in the month.”
  • “We don't feel that we have seen AI that has evaded our ability to detect the use of AI or the threats more broadly,” Smith added. “Our goal needs to be to keep AI advancing as a defensive tool faster than it advances as an offensive weapon.” 
  • The move comes after Europe has seen an uptick in cyberattacks from a number of state-sponsored groups in recent months, including those from China, Iran, Russia and North Korea.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow look for direction as job data comes in cooler than expected
data.description:
Wall Street looks for direction on Wednesday as the private sector job growth cooled more than consensus in May.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500), the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND), and the Dow (DJI) were all near even on the session.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 5 basis points to 4.41%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 4 basis points to 3.91%.

U.S. private sector employment grew by 37K in May, missing the +110K consensus and cooling from the 62K increase in April, according to data released by ADP. The pace of private hiring in May reached its lowest level since March 2023.

U.S. major averages ended Tuesday with solid gains as encouraging job openings data overshadowed a major intergovernmental organization downgrading its global and U.S. economic growth projections.

In trade news, Donald Trump said that Chinese President Xi Jinping has been “extremely hard” to strike a deal with, as the White House hinted at the possibility of the two leaders speaking this week amid escalating trade tensions.

"The mood was boosted yesterday by tech and semiconductor stocks, as Meta’s deal to enter a 20-year agreement with the largest U.S. nuclear operator seemed to energize the sector. The deal was said to be cheaper than a similar one powered by Microsoft last year, as it used existing facilities," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

One of the most important catalysts for the recovery was that JOLTS report, which showed the US labor market in a more robust position than previously thought, Reid added. 

The U.S. ISM Services PMI  retreated to 49.9 in May from 51.6 in April.

Furthermore, the S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI advanced to 53.0 in May's final estimate, compared with the 52.1 flash reading and 50.6 in April.

data.title: ISM Services PMI unexpectedly falls back into contraction in May
data.description: The U.S. ISM Services PMI retreated to 49.9 in May from 51.6 in April, missing the 52.0 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Wednesday.
